{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_1fc90e66399c498f81cee827","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp\n2\t\n3\t**A personal command interface.** Speak or type a stream-of-consciousness request — spanning multiple projects, questions, and tasks — and receive parallel, organized results on a live browser canvas or in audio.\n4\t\n5\tCLI alias: `adc`\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## How It Works\n10\t\n11\t1. **Intent routing** — a single fast LLM call segments your utterance into typed intent threads, each tagged with a project, an intent type (`status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`), and an urgency level (`critical`, `high`, `normal`, `low`).\n12\t\n13\t2. **Parallel dispatch** — all threads run concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n14\t\n15\t3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n16\t\n17\t4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n18\t\n19\t5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n20\t\n21\t```\n22\t  Voice / Text Input\n23\t  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n24\t                        │\n25\t                        ▼\n26\t             ┌──────────────────┐\n27\t             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n28\t             └──────────────────┘\n29\t                        │\n30\t          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n31\t          ▼                            ▼\n32\t   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n33\t   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n34\t          │                            │\n35\t          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n36\t                         ▼\n37\t              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n38\t              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n39\t              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n40\t              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n41\t              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n42\t              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n43\t              └─────────────────────────┘\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n47\t\n48\t---\n49\t\n50\t## Features\n51\t\n52\t### Input methods\n53\t- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n54\t- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n55\t\n56\t### Live canvas\n57\tSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n58\t\n59\t### Voice narration\n60\tResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n61\t- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n62\t- `high` — next natural pause\n63\t- `normal` — batched delivery\n64\t- `low` — only when idle\n65\t\n66\t### Self-modification and hot-reload\n67\tAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n68\t- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n69\t- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n70\t- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n71\t- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n72\t\n73\tA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n74\t\n75\t### Project registry\n76\t`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n77\t\n78\t### Session persistence\n79\tSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n80\t\n81\t---\n82\t\n83\t## Quick Start\n84\t\n85\t```bash\n86\t# Clone and install\n87\tgit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\n88\tcd aide-de-camp\n89\t\n90\tpip install -e \".[dev]\"\n91\t# or\n92\tpip install -r requirements.txt\n93\t\n94\t# Start the server\n95\tuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n96\t\n97\t# Verify it's running\n98\tcurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n99\t```\n100\t\n101\tOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n102\t\n103\tFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n104\t\n105\t---\n106\t\n107\t## CLI Usage\n108\t\n109\t```bash\n110\t# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\n111\tadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n112\t\n113\t# Query a specific topic\n114\tadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n115\t\n116\t# Current session summary\n117\tadc status\n118\t\n119\t# List active topic cards\n120\tadc topics\n121\t\n122\t# Show exception queue\n123\tadc exceptions\n124\t\n125\t# Configure the CLI\n126\tadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\n127\tadc config --set-session <session-id>\n128\t\n129\t# Help\n130\tadc --help\n131\t```\n132\t\n133\t---\n134\t\n135\t## Configuration\n136\t\n137\t### Environment variables\n138\t\n139\t| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n140\t|----------|---------|---------|\n141\t| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n142\t| `ZAI_PROXY_URL` | ZAI proxy endpoint for LLM calls (routing and synthesis) | `https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages` |\n143\t| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n144\t| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications (see note below) | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` |\n145\t| `ADC_WHISPER_STT_URL` | Whisper STT service URL for browser speech-to-text fallback | `https://whisper.ardenone.com` |\n146\t\n147\tThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configured via the `ZAI_PROXY_URL` environment variable. All LLM calls route through the ZAI proxy.\n148\t\n149\t**Telegram bridge (`ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL`) — reachability.** The value above is the Traefik `vpn`-entrypoint route for the `telegram-proxy` service on `ardenone-cluster` (IngressRoute `telegram-proxy-vpn`, namespace `telegram-bridge` → svc `telegram-proxy:8080`). The route and request contract are verified against the live service:\n150\t\n151\t- `GET /health` → `200 {\"ok\":true,\"polling\":true,\"contract_version\":\"1.0\"}`\n152\t- `POST /send` accepts `{chat_id, text, parse_mode}` (returns `405` on GET — endpoint exists, POST-only)\n153\t- `GET /send_message` → `404` (the originally-assumed path does **not** exist)\n154\t\n155\tThe fallback client in `src/telegram/fallback.py` sends to `/send` and probes `/health`, matching this contract. The in-code default (`http://telegram-claude-bridge:8000`) is the in-cluster service name for a future in-cluster deployment; set `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` to the `*-ts.ardenone.com` URL above for the local Phase-0 host.\n156\t\n157\t> **DNS caveat (verified 2026-07-19):** on this host the `*.ts.ardenone.com` split-DNS names — this bridge hostname included — do **not** resolve (`getaddrinfo` fails), while other mesh names such as the ZAI proxy do. The route itself is confirmed reachable when the name resolves (e.g. via the cluster's Tailscale egress). Until the DNS record is created, point `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` at a resolvable address or add a local/mesh-DNS mapping; otherwise the startup reachability probe fails and `/api/v1/status/telegram_bridge` reports `reachable: false`.\n158\t\n159\t### Prompts directory\n160\t\n161\tAll prompts are hot-reloaded on every invocation. Edit these files to tune behavior without restarting the server:\n162\t\n163\t| File | Purpose |\n164\t|------|---------|\n165\t| `prompts/router.md` | Intent classification and thread segmentation |\n166\t| `prompts/synthesize.md` | Result synthesis (raw data → 2–3 sentence summary) |\n167\t| `prompts/voice.md` | Narration style and pacing |\n168\t| `prompts/urgency.md` | Urgency classification rules |\n169\t\n170\t### Project registry\n171\t\n172\t`config/registry.yaml` defines the projects aide-de-camp knows about. Each entry specifies:\n173\t- `name` and `aliases` — how the router maps utterance fragments to projects\n174\t- `cluster` and `namespace` — where to run kubectl-based fetch commands\n175\t- `workflow_templates` — named multi-step workflows (e.g., \"deploy\", \"rollback\")\n176\t\n177\t---\n178\t\n179\t## Architecture\n180\t\n181\t### Key source files\n182\t\n183\t| File | Responsibility |\n184\t|------|---------------|\n185\t| `src/main.py` | FastAPI app; `/dispatch`, `/voice`, `/sse` endpoints |\n186\t| `src/intent/router.py` | LLM classification and parallel thread dispatch |\n187\t| `src/fetch/commands.py` | Fetch command matrix, intent types, data structures |\n188\t| `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` | Concurrent fetch execution with streaming and coverage tracking (FetchStrand implementation) |\n189\t| `src/synthesize/strand.py` | LLM synthesis strand |\n190\t| `src/session/store.py` | SQLite session store (aiosqlite) |\n191\t| `src/sse/broadcaster.py` | SSE connection registry and event routing |\n192\t| `src/canvas/index.html` | Canvas single-page app |\n193\t| `src/realtime/session.py` | OpenAI Realtime API voice session |\n194\t| `src/watcher/daemon.py` | Async work-item watcher — pushes completed results to canvas |\n195\t| `src/agents/self_modification.py` | Self-improvement agent |\n196\t| `src/environment/discovery.py` | Local and remote repo scanner |\n197\t| `config/registry.yaml` | Explicit project registry |\n198\t| `prompts/` | Hot-reload prompt files |\n199\t\n200\t### Runtime breakdown\n201\t\n202\t| Runtime | Used for |\n203\t|---------|---------|\n204\t| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n205\t| Configurable LLM backend | Intent router, synthesize strand |\n206\t| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git execution), bead watcher |\n207\t| Async worker fleet | UI-regen agent, self-modification agent, background analysis, task work items |\n208\t\n209\t### Data flow\n210\t\n211\t```\n212\tPOST /dispatch\n213\t  → intent router (LLM classify)\n214\t  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n215\t  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n216\t  → broadcast SSEEvent(\"result_created\", surface_id)\n217\t  → canvas calls GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics\n218\t  → cards rendered / updated in place\n219\t```\n220\t\n221\t---\n222\t\n223\t## Requirements\n224\t\n225\t- Python 3.11+\n226\t- `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `httpx`, `websockets`, `pydantic`, `aiosqlite`\n227\t- Dev: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `ruff`\n228\t\n229\t---\n230\t\n231\t## Versioning\n232\t\n233\tVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n\n3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n\n4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n\n5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n\n```\n  Voice / Text Input\n  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n                        │\n                        ▼\n             ┌──────────────────┐\n             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n             └──────────────────┘\n                        │\n          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n          ▼                            ▼\n   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n          │                            │\n          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n                         ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n              └─────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n\n---\n\n## Features\n\n### Input methods\n- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n\n### Live canvas\nSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n\n### Voice narration\nResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n- `high` — next natural pause\n- `normal` — batched delivery\n- `low` — only when idle\n\n### Self-modification and hot-reload\nAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n\nA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n\n### Project registry\n`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n\n### Session persistence\nSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Clone and install\ngit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\ncd aide-de-camp\n\npip install -e \".[dev]\"\n# or\npip install -r requirements.txt\n\n# Start the server\nuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n\n# Verify it's running\ncurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n\nFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n\n---\n\n## CLI Usage\n\n```bash\n# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\nadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n\n# Query a specific topic\nadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n\n# Current session summary\nadc status\n\n# List active topic cards\nadc topics\n\n# Show exception queue\nadc exceptions\n\n# Configure the CLI\nadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\nadc config --set-session <session-id>\n\n# Help\nadc --help\n```\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Environment variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n| `ZAI_PROXY_URL` | ZAI proxy endpoint for LLM calls (routing and synthesis) | `https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages` |\n| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications (see note below) | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` |\n| `ADC_WHISPER_STT_URL` | Whisper STT service URL for browser speech-to-text fallback | `https://whisper.ardenone.com` |\n\nThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configured via the `ZAI_PROXY_URL` environment variable. AND component_id = ?\n            ORDER BY layout_bucket\n            \"\"\",\n            (\"status:test\", sample_component.id)\n        ).fetchall()\n\n        assert len(rows) == 3\n\n        # Create a map for verification (ordering may vary)\n        layout_map = {row[0]: row[1] for row in rows}\n\n        # Verify all layouts are present\n        assert 'compact' in layout_map\n        assert 'normal' in layout_map\n        assert 'expanded' in layout_map\n\n        # Verify the scores were stored correctly\n        expected_scores = {\n            'compact': 0.80,\n            'normal': 0.85,\n            'expanded': 0.90\n        }\n        for layout, score in layout_map.items():\n            expected = expected_scores[layout]\n            assert abs(score - expected) < 0.01\n\n    def test_record_usage_pattern_updates_existing(self, library, sample_component):\n        \"\"\"Test that recording the same pattern updates existing entry.\"\"\"\n        # First recording\n        library.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=sample_component.id,\n            result_type=\"status:test\",\n            match_score=0.80,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n        )\n\n        time.sleep(0.1)  # Small delay to ensure updated_at changes\n\n        # Second recording with different score\n        library.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=sample_component.id,\n            result_type=\"status:test\",\n            match_score=0.90,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n        )\n\n        conn = library._get_conn()\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT match_score, sample_count, updated_at\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE result_type = ? AND component_id = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (\"status:test\", sample_component.id, \"normal\")\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        assert row is not None\n        # Match score should be averaged: (0.80 * 1 + 0.90) / 2 = 0.85\n        assert abs(row[0] - 0.85) < 0.01  # Allow for floating point errors\n        assert row[1] == 2  # sample_count incremented\n\n    def test_select_component_by_result_type(self, library, sample_component):\n        \"\"\"Test selecting component by result_type.\"\"\"\n        # Record a pattern\n        library.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=sample_component.id,\n            result_type=\"status:test\",\n            match_score=0.95,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n        )\n\n        # Select component\n        component = library.select_component_for_result_type(\n            result_type=\"status:test\",\n            match_threshold=0.7,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n        )\n\n        assert component is not None\n        assert component.id == sample_component.id\n\n    def test_select_component_below_threshold(self, library, sample_component):\n        \"\"\"Test that components below threshold are not selected.\"\"\"\n        # {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_4e23d05f6bdf4ed9984d64d5","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Component Usage Patterns Seed Data Design\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThis document defines the seed data structure and match score values for the `component_usage_patterns` table in the aide-de-camp component library system. The seed data provides initial mappings between result types and UI components to enable the hot-path renderer to select appropriate components without LLM calls.\n6\t\n7\t## Schema Definition\n8\t\n9\t### Table: `component_usage_patterns`\n10\t\n11\t```sql\n12\tCREATE TABLE component_usage_patterns (\n13\t    result_type TEXT NOT NULL,          -- e.g., \"status:ibkr-mcp\", \"lookup:logs:adc\"\n14\t    component_id TEXT NOT NULL,        -- References components.id\n15\t    layout_bucket TEXT NOT NULL,        -- 'compact' | 'normal' | 'expanded'\n16\t    match_score REAL NOT NULL,          -- Confidence score 0.0-1.0\n17\t    sample_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,\n18\t    updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL,        -- Unix timestamp\n19\t    PRIMARY KEY (result_type, component_id, layout_bucket)\n20\t);\n21\t```\n22\t\n23\t### Seed Data Format\n24\t\n25\t```python\n26\t(\n27\t    result_type: str,      # Colon-separated hierarchical key\n28\t    component_id: str,      # Must exist in components table\n29\t    layout_bucket: str,    # One of: 'compact', 'normal', 'expanded'\n30\t    match_score: float,    # 0.0 to 1.0 confidence\n31\t    sample_count: int      # Number of times this pattern has been observed\n32\t)\n33\t```\n34\t\n35\t## Result Type Taxonomy\n36\t\n37\tResult types follow a colon-separated hierarchical naming convention derived in `src/render/hot_path.py`:\n38\t\n39\t```\n40\t{intent_type}:{project_slug}                    # Intent-derived\n41\tlookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}            # Lookup threads\n42\tmonitoring:{project_slug}                       # Monitoring results\n43\t{intent_type}:general                           # No project slug fallback\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t### Common Intent Types\n47\t\n48\t- **status** - Status checks, health queries, state queries\n49\t- **action** - Actions, operations, executions\n50\t- **lookup** - Data lookups (logs, config, metrics)\n51\t- **monitoring** - Monitoring and alerting data\n52\t- **compound** - Multi-part aggregated results\n53\t\n54\t### Common Project Slugs\n55\t\n56\t- **ibkr-mcp** - Interactive Brokers MCP server\n57\t- **adc** - aide-de-camp (self)\n58\t- **general** - Fallback when no project identified\n59\t- **k8s** - Kubernetes operations\n60\t- **git** - Git operations\n61\t\n62\t## Layout Buckets\n63\t\n64\t### Layout: `compact` (Dense, ~120px height)\n65\t\n66\t- **Use case**: High-density displays, list views, status dashboards\n67\t- **Best for**: Simple status checks, binary states, counters, badges\n68\t- **Typical content**: Status indicator + 1-2 key fields, minimal text\n69\t- **Examples**: Pod running/stopped, build success/fail, deployment ready/pending\n70\t\n71\t### Layout: `normal` (Balanced, ~200px height)\n72\t\n73\t- **Use case**: Default card layout, balanced information density\n74\t- **Best for**: Most result types, moderate information density\n75\t- **Typical content**: Title + 3-6 key-value pairs, summary, 1-level nested data\n76\t- **Examples**: Pod status with resource limits, commit info with files changed, logs viewer preview\n77\t\n78\t### Layout: `expanded` (Full, ~400px+ height)\n79\t\n80\t- **Use case**: Maximum information display, detailed views\n81\t- **Best for**: Complex results, multi-item lists, full logs/config\n82\t- **Typical content**: Full arrays/objects, long text, multiple sections, tables\n83\t- **Examples**: Full pod spec, complete commit message, extended logs, full config file\n84\t\n85\t## Match Score Rationale\n86\t\n87\t### Score Range: 0.0 - 1.0\n88\t\n89\t| Score Range | Confidence | Interpretation |\n90\t|-------------|-----------|----------------|\n91\t| **0.95 - 1.00** | Perfect | Exact component designed for this result type |\n92\t| **0.85 - 0.94** | Excellent | Very strong semantic match, purpose-built component |\n93\t| **0.70 - 0.84** | Good | Strong match, general-purpose component fits well |\n94\t| **0.50 - 0.69** | Fair | Moderate match, usable but not ideal |\n95\t| **0.30 - 0.49** | Poor | Weak match, fallback territory |\n96\t| **0.00 - 0.29** | None | No meaningful match, should not be used |\n97\t\n98\t### Scoring Guidelines\n99\t\n100\t1. **Exact name match** = 1.0 (component.name == result_type key part)\n101\t2. **Keyword overlap** = +0.1 per matching keyword (max +0.3)\n102\t3. **Domain specificity** = +0.15 for domain-specific components\n103\t4. **Layout appropriateness** = +0.05 for layout-appropriate components\n104\t5. **Proven fit** = baseline score from successful usage\n105\t\n106\t### Thresholds\n107\t\n108\t- **Hot-path threshold**: 0.7 (DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD in `hot_path.py`)\n109\t- **Semantic fallback threshold**: 0.5 (in `ComponentLibrary.find_best_component`)\n110\t- Patterns below 0.7 will trigger fallback to generic card\n111\t\n112\t## Seed Data Patterns\n113\t\n114\t### Core Principle\n115\t\n116\tEach pattern represents a learned mapping: *\"When result_type X appears in context Y, component Z with layout L is a good fit with confidence S.\"*\n117\t\n118\t### Category: Status Results\n119\t\n120\t```python\n121\t# Project-specific status patterns\n122\t(\"status:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-ibkr-status\", \"normal\", 0.95, 25),\n123\t(\"status:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-ibkr-status\", \"compact\", 0.90, 10),\n124\t(\"status:adc\", \"comp-adc-status\", \"normal\", 0.95, 20),\n125\t(\"status:adc\", \"comp-adc-status\", \"compact\", 0.88, 8),\n126\t\n127\t# Kubernetes status patterns\n128\t(\"status:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-pod-status\", \"normal\", 0.94, 30),\n129\t(\"status:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-pod-status\", \"compact\", 0.89, 15),\n130\t(\"status:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-deployment-status\", \"normal\", 0.92, 18),\n131\t(\"status:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-service-status\", \"compact\", 0.91, 12),\n132\t\n133\t# Git operations status\n134\t(\"status:git\", \"comp-git-status\", \"normal\", 0.93, 22),\n135\t(\"status:git\", \"comp-git-status\", \"compact\", 0.87, 11),\n136\t(\"status:git\", \"comp-git-commit-info\", \"normal\", 0.91, 16),\n137\t\n138\t# CI/CD status\n139\t(\"status:ci\", \"comp-ci-pipeline-status\", \"normal\", 0.96, 28),\n140\t(\"status:ci\", \"comp-ci-pipeline-status\", \"compact\", 0.90, 14),\n141\t(\"status:ci\", \"comp-ci-build-status\", \"expanded\", 0.92, 10),\n142\t```\n143\t\n144\t**Rationale**:\n145\t- Project-specific patterns get highest scores (0.95) for exact fit\n146\t- K8s patterns score high (0.89-0.94) as k8s operations have well-defined schemas\n147\t- CI/CD gets 0.92-0.96 as CI results are highly structured\n148\t- Compact layouts score 0.04-0.06 lower than normal for same pattern (density trade-off)\n149\t\n150\t### Category: Lookup Results\n151\t\n152\t```python\n153\t# Logs lookup patterns\n154\t(\"lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-logs-viewer\", \"expanded\", 0.96, 40),\n155\t(\"lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-logs-viewer\", \"normal\", 0.93, 25),\n156\t(\"lookup:logs:adc\", \"comp-logs-viewer\", \"expanded\", 0.95, 35),\n157\t(\"lookup:logs:adc\", \"comp-logs-viewer\", \"normal\", 0.92, 20),\n158\t(\"lookup:logs:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-logs\", \"expanded\", 0.97, 50),\n159\t(\"lookup:logs:general\", \"comp-logs-viewer\", \"expanded\", 0.85, 15),\n160\t\n161\t# Config lookup patterns\n162\t(\"lookup:config:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-config-viewer\", \"normal\", 0.92, 18),\n163\t(\"lookup:config:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-config-viewer\", \"expanded\", 0.94, 12),\n164\t(\"lookup:config:adc\", \"comp-config-viewer\", \"normal\", 0.91, 15),\n165\t(\"lookup:config:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-config\", \"expanded\", 0.95, 30),\n166\t(\"lookup:config:general\", \"comp-config-viewer\", \"normal\", 0.82, 10),\n167\t\n168\t# Metrics lookup patterns\n169\t(\"lookup:metrics:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-metrics\", \"normal\", 0.93, 20),\n170\t(\"lookup:metrics:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-metrics\", \"expanded\", 0.95, 15),\n171\t```\n172\t\n173\t**Rationale**:\n174\t- Logs patterns score highest (0.93-0.97) as logs have very specific structure\n175\t- Expanded layouts favored for logs (full log display)\n176\t- Config scores 0.82-0.95 depending on specificity\n177\t- Metrics patterns get 0.93+ for structured numerical data\n178\t\n179\t### Category: Action Results\n180\t\n181\t```python\n182\t# General action patterns\n183\t(\"action:general\", \"comp-action-result\", \"normal\", 0.85, 20),\n184\t(\"action:general\", \"comp-action-result\", \"compact\", 0.80, 10),\n185\t\n186\t# Deployment actions\n187\t(\"action:deploy\", \"comp-deployment-result\", \"normal\", 0.92, 25),\n188\t(\"action:deploy\", \"comp-deployment-result\", \"expanded\", 0.90, 12),\n189\t\n190\t# Restart/restart actions\n191\t(\"action:restart\", \"comp-restart-result\", \"compact\", 0.91, 18),\n192\t(\"action:restart\", \"comp-restart-result\", \"normal\", 0.88, 14),\n193\t\n194\t# Scaling actions\n195\t(\"action:scale\", \"comp-scale-result\", \"normal\", 0.90, 16),\n196\t(\"action:scale\", \"comp-scale-result\", \"expanded\", 0.87, 8),\n197\t```\n198\t\n199\t**Rationale**:\n200\t- General actions get moderate scores (0.80-0.85) as action results vary widely\n201\t- Specific action types (deploy, restart, scale) score higher (0.87-0.92)\n202\t- Compact layouts work well for simple binary action results\n203\t\n204\t### Category: Monitoring Results\n205\t\n206\t```python\n207\t# Kubernetes monitoring\n208\t(\"monitoring:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-monitoring\", \"normal\", 0.94, 35),\n209\t(\"monitoring:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-monitoring\", \"expanded\", 0.96, 25),\n210\t(\"monitoring:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-monitoring\", \"compact\", 0.88, 15),\n211\t\n212\t# Application monitoring\n213\t(\"monitoring:adc\", \"comp-adc-metrics\", \"normal\", 0.91, 20),\n214\t(\"monitoring:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-ibkr-metrics\", \"normal\", 0.90, 18),\n215\t\n216\t# General monitoring\n217\t(\"monitoring:general\", \"comp-monitoring-card\", \"normal\", 0.82, 12),\n218\t```\n219\t\n220\t**Rationale**:\n221\t- K8s monitoring scores highest (0.88-0.96) as k8s metrics are well-structured\n222\t- Expanded layouts score highest for monitoring (time series, multiple metrics)\n223\t- Project-specific monitoring scores 0.90-0.91\n224\t\n225\t### Category: Fallback Patterns (Generic Components)\n226\t\n227\t```python\n228\t# Generic fallbacks for unmatched types\n229\t(\"status:general\", \"comp-generic-status\", \"normal\", 0.70, 30),\n230\t(\"status:general\", \"comp-generic-status\", \"compact\", 0.68, 15),\n231\t(\"action:general\", \"comp-generic-action\", \"normal\", 0.70, 25),\n232\t(\"lookup:general\", \"comp-generic-lookup\", \"normal\", 0.70, 20),\n233\t(\"monitoring:general\", \"comp-generic-monitoring\", \"normal\", 0.70, 18),\n234\t(\"compound:general\", \"comp-generic-compound\", \"expanded\", 0.70, 22),\n235\t```\n236\t\n237\t**Rationale**:\n238\t- All generic patterns score exactly 0.70 (at threshold)\n239\t- Provides minimum viable match without false confidence\n240\t- Sample counts reflect high usage but low specificity\n241\t\n242\t## Component Template Examples\n243\t\n244\t### Status Component Template\n245\t\n246\t```html\n247\t<!-- comp-k8s-pod-status -->\n248\t<div class=\"k8s-pod-card\" data-pod-name=\"{{pod.name}}\" data-namespace=\"{{pod.namespace}}\">\n249\t  <div class=\"pod-header\">\n250\t    <span class=\"pod-name\">{{pod.name}}</span>\n251\t    <span class=\"pod-namespace\">{{pod.namespace}}</span>\n252\t  </div>\n253\t  <div class=\"pod-status {{pod.phase.lower}}\">{{pod.phase}}</span>\n254\t  <div class=\"pod-details\">\n255\t    <div class=\"detail-row\">\n256\t      <span class=\"label\">Ready:</span>\n257\t      <span class=\"value\">{{pod.ready}}/{{pod.containers}}</span>\n258\t    </div>\n259\t    <div class=\"detail-row\">\n260\t      <span class=\"label\">Restarts:</span>\n261\t      <span class=\"value\">{{pod.restarts}}</span>\n262\t    </div>\n263\t    <div class=\"detail-row\">\n264\t      <span class=\"label\">Age:</span>\n265\t      <span class=\"value\">{{pod.age}}</span>\n266\t    </div>\n267\t  </div>\n268\t</div>\n269\t```\n270\t\n271\t### Logs Component Template\n272\t\n273\t```html\n274\t<!-- comp-logs-viewer -->\n275\t<div class=\"logs-viewer\" data-source=\"{{source}}\">\n276\t  <div class=\"logs-header\">\n277\t    <span class=\"source\">{{source}}</span>\n278\t    <span class=\"line-count\">{{lines.length}} lines</span>\n279\t  </div>\n280\t  <div class=\"logs-content\">\n281\t    {{#each lines}}\n282\t    <div class=\"log-line {{#if error}}error{{/if}}\">\n283\t      <span class=\"timestamp\">{{this.timestamp}}</span>\n284\t      <span class=\"level\">{{this.level}}</span>\n285\t      <span class=\"message\">{{this.message}}</span>\n286\t    </div>\n287\t    {{/each}}\n288\t  </div>\n289\t</div>\n290\t```\n291\t\n292\t## Sample Count Guidance\n293\t\n294\tSample counts should reflect:\n295\t\n296\t1. **Production usage**: 20-50 for well-established patterns\n297\t2. **Common patterns**: 10-25 for frequently used patterns\n298\t3. **New patterns**: 5-15 for recently established patterns\n299\t4. **Generic fallbacks**: 15-30 (high usage, low specificity)\n300\t\n301\tHigher sample counts indicate more reliable patterns when sorting by `match_score DESC, sample_count DESC`.\n302\t\n303\t## Layout Bucket Assignment Strategy\n304\t\n305\t### When to use `compact`:\n306\t\n307\t- Binary status (running/stopped, success/fail)\n308\t- Single-value metrics (CPU: 75%, Memory: 2GB)\n309\t- Counters and badges (3 pods, 5 errors)\n310\t- High-density status dashboards\n311\t\n312\t### When to use `normal`:\n313\t\n314\t- Default layout for most result types\n315\t- 3-8 key-value pairs\n316\t- Single-level nested objects\n317\t- Summary + details views\n318\t\n319\t### When to use `expanded`:\n320\t\n321\t- Logs and text output (>10 lines)\n322\t- Full configurations (YAML/JSON)\n323\t- Multi-item lists (>10 items)\n324\t- Time-series data\n325\t- Complex nested structures\n326\t\n327\t## Implementation Notes\n328\t\n329\t1. **Component ID Requirements**: All `component_id` values must exist in the `components` table before seed patterns are loaded. The `load_seed_patterns()` function in `seed_patterns.py` validates this.\n330\t\n331\t2. **Timestamp Handling**: The `updated_at` field uses Unix timestamps (seconds since epoch). Seed data should use `int(time.time())` at load time.\n332\t\n333\t3. **Transaction Safety**: Pattern loading uses `INSERT OR IGNORE` to prevent conflicts with existing patterns while being idempotent.\n334\t\n335\t4. **Validation**: Match scores are validated to be in range 0.0-1.0. Layout buckets must be one of: 'compact', 'normal', 'expanded'.\n336\t\n337\t5. **Index Usage**: The `idx_component_usage_patterns_match_score` index enables efficient queries for `ORDER BY match_score DESC`.\n338\t\n339\t## Usage Example\n340\t\n341\t```python\n342\tfrom src.components.seed_patterns import load_seed_patterns, DEFAULT_SEED_PATTERNS\n343\tfrom src.components.library import get_library\n344\t\n345\t# Load seed patterns into the component library\n346\tlibrary = get_library()\n347\tloaded_count = load_seed_patterns(library)\n348\t\n349\tprint(f\"Loaded {loaded_count} seed patterns\")\n350\t```\n351\t\n352\t## Migration Notes\n353\t\n354\tWhen migrating from the old schema (without `layout_bucket`), existing patterns are assigned `layout_bucket = 'normal'` by default. See `_migrate_component_usage_patterns()` in `library.py`.\n355\t\n356\t---\n357\t\n358\t**Document Version**: 1.0  \n359\t**Last Updated**: 2025-01-23  \n360\t**Schema Version**: component_usage_patterns v2 (with layout_bucket and updated_at)\n361\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bf03f106-b4b9-4b48-9055-caeb6ae3e232","uuid":"5cc0054e-7081-4687-9c61-a93590f37797","timestamp":"2026-07-23T17:08:51.965Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/design/component_usage_patterns_seed_data.md","content":"# Component Usage Patterns Seed Data Design\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document defines the seed data structure and match score values for the `component_usage_patterns` table in the aide-de-camp component library system. The seed data provides initial mappings between result types and UI components to enable the hot-path renderer to select appropriate components without LLM calls.\n\n## Schema Definition\n\n### Table: `component_usage_patterns`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE component_usage_patterns (\n    result_type TEXT NOT NULL,          -- e.g., \"status:ibkr-mcp\", \"lookup:logs:adc\"\n    component_id TEXT NOT NULL,        -- References components.id\n    layout_bucket TEXT NOT NULL,        -- 'compact' | 'normal' | 'expanded'\n    match_score REAL NOT NULL,          -- Confidence score 0.0-1.0\n    sample_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,\n    updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL,        -- Unix timestamp\n    PRIMARY KEY (result_type, component_id, layout_bucket)\n);\n```\n\n### Seed Data Format\n\n```python\n(\n    result_type: str,      # Colon-separated hierarchical key\n    component_id: str,      # Must exist in components table\n    layout_bucket: str,    # One of: 'compact', 'normal', 'expanded'\n    match_score: float,    # 0.0 to 1.0 confidence\n    sample_count: int      # Number of times this pattern has been observed\n)\n```\n\n## Result Type Taxonomy\n\nResult types follow a colon-separated hierarchical naming convention derived in `src/render/hot_path.py`:\n\n```\n{intent_type}:{project_slug}                    # Intent-derived\nlookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}            # Lookup threads\nmonitoring:{project_slug}                       # Monitoring results\n{intent_type}:general                           # No project slug fallback\n```\n\n### Common Intent Types\n\n- **status** - Status checks, health queries, state queries\n- **action** - Actions, operations, executions\n- **lookup** - Data lookups (logs, config, metrics)\n- **monitoring** - Monitoring and alerting data\n- **compound** - Multi-part aggregated results\n\n### Common Project Slugs\n\n- **ibkr-mcp** - Interactive Brokers MCP server\n- **adc** - aide-de-camp (self)\n- **general** - Fallback when no project identified\n- **k8s** - Kubernetes operations\n- **git** - Git operations\n\n## Layout Buckets\n\n### Layout: `compact` (Dense, ~120px height)\n\n- **Use case**: High-density displays, list views, status dashboards\n- **Best for**: Simple status checks, binary states, counters, badges\n- **Typical content**: Status indicator + 1-2 key fields, minimal text\n- **Examples**: Pod running/stopped, build success/fail, deployment ready/pending\n\n### Layout: `normal` (Balanced, ~200px height)\n\n- **Use case**: Default card layout, balanced information density\n- **Best for**: Most result types, moderate information density\n- **Typical content**: Title + 3-6 key-value pairs, summary, 1-level nested data\n- **Examples**: Pod status with resource limits, commit info with files changed, logs viewer preview\n\n### Layout: `expanded` (Full, ~400px+ height)\n\n- **Use case**: Maximum information display, detailed views\n- **Best for**: Complex results, multi-item lists, full logs/config\n- **Typical content**: Full arrays/objects, long text, multiple sections, tables\n- **Examples**: Full pod spec, complete commit message, extended logs, full config file\n\n## Match Score Rationale\n\n### Score Range: 0.0 - 1.0\n\n| Score Range | Confidence | Interpretation |\n|-------------|-----------|----------------|\n| **0.95 - 1.00** | Perfect | Exact component designed for this result type |\n| **0.85 - 0.94** | Excellent | Very strong semantic match, purpose-built component |\n| **0.70 - 0.84** | Good | Strong match, general-purpose component fits well |\n| **0.50 - 0.69** | Fair | Moderate match, usable but not ideal |\n| **0.30 - 0.49** | Poor | Weak match, fallback territory |\n| **0.00 - 0.29** | None | No meaningful match, should not be used |\n\n### Scoring Guidelines\n\n1. **Exact name match** = 1.0 (component.name == result_type key part)\n2. **Keyword overlap** = +0.1 per matching keyword (max +0.3)\n3. **Domain specificity** = +0.15 for domain-specific components\n4. **Layout appropriateness** = +0.05 for layout-appropriate components\n5. **Proven fit** = baseline score from successful usage\n\n### Thresholds\n\n- **Hot-path threshold**: 0.7 (DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD in `hot_path.py`)\n- **Semantic fallback threshold**: 0.5 (in `ComponentLibrary.find_best_component`)\n- Patterns below 0.7 will trigger fallback to generic card\n\n## Seed Data Patterns\n\n### Core Principle\n\nEach pattern represents a learned mapping: *\"When result_type X appears in context Y, component Z with layout L is a good fit with confidence S.\"*\n\n### Category: Status Results\n\n```python\n# Project-specific status patterns\n(\"status:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-ibkr-status\", \"normal\", 0.95, 25),\n(\"status:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-ibkr-status\", \"compact\", 0.90, 10),\n(\"status:adc\", \"comp-adc-status\", \"normal\", 0.95, 20),\n(\"status:adc\", \"comp-adc-status\", \"compact\", 0.88, 8),\n\n# Kubernetes status patterns\n(\"status:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-pod-status\", \"normal\", 0.94, 30),\n(\"status:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-pod-status\", \"compact\", 0.89, 15),\n(\"status:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-deployment-status\", \"normal\", 0.92, 18),\n(\"status:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-service-status\", \"compact\", 0.91, 12),\n\n# Git operations status\n(\"status:git\", \"comp-git-status\", \"normal\", 0.93, 22),\n(\"status:git\", \"comp-git-status\", \"compact\", 0.87, 11),\n(\"status:git\", \"comp-git-commit-info\", \"normal\", 0.91, 16),\n\n# CI/CD status\n(\"status:ci\", \"comp-ci-pipeline-status\", \"normal\", 0.96, 28),\n(\"status:ci\", \"comp-ci-pipeline-status\", \"compact\", 0.90, 14),\n(\"status:ci\", \"comp-ci-build-status\", \"expanded\", 0.92, 10),\n```\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Project-specific patterns get highest scores (0.95) for exact fit\n- K8s patterns score high (0.89-0.94) as k8s operations have well-defined schemas\n- CI/CD gets 0.92-0.96 as CI results are highly structured\n- Compact layouts score 0.04-0.06 lower than normal for same pattern (density trade-off)\n\n### Category: Lookup Results\n\n```python\n# Logs lookup patterns\n(\"lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-logs-viewer\", \"expanded\", 0.96, 40),\n(\"lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-logs-viewer\", \"normal\", 0.93, 25),\n(\"lookup:logs:adc\", \"comp-logs-viewer\", \"expanded\", 0.95, 35),\n(\"lookup:logs:adc\", \"comp-logs-viewer\", \"normal\", 0.92, 20),\n(\"lookup:logs:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-logs\", \"expanded\", 0.97, 50),\n(\"lookup:logs:general\", \"comp-logs-viewer\", \"expanded\", 0.85, 15),\n\n# Config lookup patterns\n(\"lookup:config:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-config-viewer\", \"normal\", 0.92, 18),\n(\"lookup:config:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-config-viewer\", \"expanded\", 0.94, 12),\n(\"lookup:config:adc\", \"comp-config-viewer\", \"normal\", 0.91, 15),\n(\"lookup:config:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-config\", \"expanded\", 0.95, 30),\n(\"lookup:config:general\", \"comp-config-viewer\", \"normal\", 0.82, 10),\n\n# Metrics lookup patterns\n(\"lookup:metrics:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-metrics\", \"normal\", 0.93, 20),\n(\"lookup:metrics:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-metrics\", \"expanded\", 0.95, 15),\n```\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Logs patterns score highest (0.93-0.97) as logs have very specific structure\n- Expanded layouts favored for logs (full log display)\n- Config scores 0.82-0.95 depending on specificity\n- Metrics patterns get 0.93+ for structured numerical data\n\n### Category: Action Results\n\n```python\n# General action patterns\n(\"action:general\", \"comp-action-result\", \"normal\", 0.85, 20),\n(\"action:general\", \"comp-action-result\", \"compact\", 0.80, 10),\n\n# Deployment actions\n(\"action:deploy\", \"comp-deployment-result\", \"normal\", 0.92, 25),\n(\"action:deploy\", \"comp-deployment-result\", \"expanded\", 0.90, 12),\n\n# Restart/restart actions\n(\"action:restart\", \"comp-restart-result\", \"compact\", 0.91, 18),\n(\"action:restart\", \"comp-restart-result\", \"normal\", 0.88, 14),\n\n# Scaling actions\n(\"action:scale\", \"comp-scale-result\", \"normal\", 0.90, 16),\n(\"action:scale\", \"comp-scale-result\", \"expanded\", 0.87, 8),\n```\n\n**Rationale**:\n- General actions get moderate scores (0.80-0.85) as action results vary widely\n- Specific action types (deploy, restart, scale) score higher (0.87-0.92)\n- Compact layouts work well for simple binary action results\n\n### Category: Monitoring Results\n\n```python\n# Kubernetes monitoring\n(\"monitoring:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-monitoring\", \"normal\", 0.94, 35),\n(\"monitoring:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-monitoring\", \"expanded\", 0.96, 25),\n(\"monitoring:k8s\", \"comp-k8s-monitoring\", \"compact\", 0.88, 15),\n\n# Application monitoring\n(\"monitoring:adc\", \"comp-adc-metrics\", \"normal\", 0.91, 20),\n(\"monitoring:ibkr-mcp\", \"comp-ibkr-metrics\", \"normal\", 0.90, 18),\n\n# General monitoring\n(\"monitoring:general\", \"comp-monitoring-card\", \"normal\", 0.82, 12),\n```\n\n**Rationale**:\n- K8s monitoring scores highest (0.88-0.96) as k8s metrics are well-structured\n- Expanded layouts score highest for monitoring (time series, multiple metrics)\n- Project-specific monitoring scores 0.90-0.91\n\n### Category: Fallback Patterns (Generic Components)\n\n```python\n# Generic fallbacks for unmatched types\n(\"status:general\", \"comp-generic-status\", \"normal\", 0.70, 30),\n(\"status:general\", \"comp-generic-status\", \"compact\", 0.68, 15),\n(\"action:general\", \"comp-generic-action\", \"normal\", 0.70, 25),\n(\"lookup:general\", \"comp-generic-lookup\", \"normal\", 0.70, 20),\n(\"monitoring:general\", \"comp-generic-monitoring\", \"normal\", 0.70, 18),\n(\"compound:general\", \"comp-generic-compound\", \"expanded\", 0.70, 22),\n```\n\n**Rationale**:\n- All generic patterns score exactly 0.70 (at threshold)\n- Provides minimum viable match without false confidence\n- Sample counts reflect high usage but low specificity\n\n## Component Template Examples\n\n### Status Component Template\n\n```html\n<!-- comp-k8s-pod-status -->\n<div class=\"k8s-pod-card\" data-pod-name=\"{{pod.name}}\" data-namespace=\"{{pod.namespace}}\">\n  <div class=\"pod-header\">\n    <span class=\"pod-name\">{{pod.name}}</span>\n    <span class=\"pod-namespace\">{{pod.namespace}}</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"pod-status {{pod.phase.lower}}\">{{pod.phase}}</span>\n  <div class=\"pod-details\">\n    <div class=\"detail-row\">\n      <span class=\"label\">Ready:</span>\n      <span class=\"value\">{{pod.ready}}/{{pod.containers}}</span>\n    </div>\n    <div class=\"detail-row\">\n      <span class=\"label\">Restarts:</span>\n      <span class=\"value\">{{pod.restarts}}</span>\n    </div>\n    <div class=\"detail-row\">\n      <span class=\"label\">Age:</span>\n      <span class=\"value\">{{pod.age}}</span>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n</div>\n```\n\n### Logs Component Template\n\n```html\n<!-- comp-logs-viewer -->\n<div class=\"logs-viewer\" data-source=\"{{source}}\">\n  <div class=\"logs-header\">\n    <span class=\"source\">{{source}}</span>\n    <span class=\"line-count\">{{lines.length}} lines</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"logs-content\">\n    {{#each lines}}\n    <div class=\"log-line {{#if error}}error{{/if}}\">\n      <span class=\"timestamp\">{{this.timestamp}}</span>\n      <span class=\"level\">{{this.level}}</span>\n      <span class=\"message\">{{this.message}}</span>\n    </div>\n    {{/each}}\n  </div>\n</div>\n```\n\n## Sample Count Guidance\n\nSample counts should reflect:\n\n1. **Production usage**: 20-50 for well-established patterns\n2. **Common patterns**: 10-25 for frequently used patterns\n3. **New patterns**: 5-15 for recently established patterns\n4. **Generic fallbacks**: 15-30 (high usage, low specificity)\n\nHigher sample counts indicate more reliable patterns when sorting by `match_score DESC, sample_count DESC`.\n\n## Layout Bucket Assignment Strategy\n\n### When to use `compact`:\n\n- Binary status (running/stopped, success/fail)\n- Single-value metrics (CPU: 75%, Memory: 2GB)\n- Counters and badges (3 pods, 5 errors)\n- High-density status dashboards\n\n### When to use `normal`:\n\n- Default layout for most result types\n- 3-8 key-value pairs\n- Single-level nested objects\n- Summary + details views\n\n### When to use `expanded`:\n\n- Logs and text output (>10 lines)\n- Full configurations (YAML/JSON)\n- Multi-item lists (>10 items)\n- Time-series data\n- Complex nested structures\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n1. **Component ID Requirements**: All `component_id` values must exist in the `components` table before seed patterns are loaded. The `load_seed_patterns()` function in `seed_patterns.py` validates this.\n\n2. **Timestamp Handling**: The `updated_at` field uses Unix timestamps (seconds since epoch). Seed data should use `int(time.time())` at load time.\n\n3. **Transaction Safety**: Pattern loading uses `INSERT OR IGNORE` to prevent conflicts with existing patterns while being idempotent.\n\n4. **Validation**: Match scores are validated to be in range 0.0-1.0. Layout buckets must be one of: 'compact', 'normal', 'expanded'.\n\n5. **Index Usage**: The `idx_component_usage_patterns_match_score` index enables efficient queries for `ORDER BY match_score DESC`.\n\n## Usage Example\n\n```python\nfrom src.components.seed_patterns import load_seed_patterns, DEFAULT_SEED_PATTERNS\nfrom src.components.library import get_library\n\n# Load seed patterns into the component library\nlibrary = get_library()\nloaded_count = load_seed_patterns(library)\n\nprint(f\"Loaded {loaded_count} seed patterns\")\n```\n\n## Migration Notes\n\nWhen migrating from the old schema (without `layout_bucket`), existing patterns are assigned `layout_bucket = 'normal'` by default. See `_migrate_component_usage_patterns()` in `library.py`.\n\n---\n\n**Document Version**: 1.0  \n**Last Updated**: 2025-01-23  \n**Schema Version**: component_usage_patterns v2 (with layout_bucket and updated_at)\n","numLines":361,"startLine":1,"totalLines":361}}}
